label: Azure Monitor
description: Query Azure Monitor metrics, logs, and Application Insights telemetry using a service principal.

form.title: Connect Azure Monitor
form.subtitle: Authorize Anton to query Azure Monitor metrics, logs, and Application Insights via a service principal.

methods.label: Service Principal (Client Credentials)
methods.description: Paste your Azure tenant ID, client ID, client secret, and subscription ID. This is the standard non-interactive auth path for Azure and works with any Azure AD-registered app that has the Monitoring Reader role.
methods.how_to:
## Service Principal — Client Credentials

This is the recommended path for connecting Azure Monitor to Anton. You register an app in Azure Active Directory, assign it the **Monitoring Reader** role on your subscription, and paste the credentials below.

## Steps

1. Open the [Azure Portal](https://portal.azure.com) and navigate to **Azure Active Directory → App registrations → New registration**.
2. Give the app a name (e.g. `anton-monitor`), leave the redirect URI blank, and click **Register**.
3. On the app's overview page, copy the **Application (client) ID** and the **Directory (tenant) ID** — paste them into the fields below.
4. Go to **Certificates & secrets → Client secrets → New client secret**. Set an expiry (12–24 months recommended), click **Add**, and immediately copy the **Value** shown — it is displayed only once.
5. Navigate to **Subscriptions**, open the subscription you want Anton to monitor, and copy the **Subscription ID**.
6. In that same subscription, go to **Access control (IAM) → Add role assignment**. Select the **Monitoring Reader** built-in role, assign it to the app you just registered, and save.
7. If you also need Application Insights query access, add the **Reader** role at the resource group or Application Insights resource level as well.
8. Paste all four values into the fields below and click **Submit**.

## Notes

- The client secret is shown only once — if you lose it, generate a new one in **Certificates & secrets** and update the field in Anton.
- For Log Analytics workspace queries, ensure the workspace's **Access control mode** allows workspace-level permissions, or grant the service principal the **Log Analytics Reader** role on the workspace.
- Anton queries the Azure Monitor REST API (`management.azure.com`) and the Log Analytics API (`api.loganalytics.io`) — ensure no network policy blocks outbound access to these endpoints.

fields.name: tenant_id
fields.label: Tenant ID
fields.description: Your Azure Active Directory tenant ID. Found under Azure Active Directory → Overview → Directory ID.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: Client ID
fields.description: The Application (client) ID of the registered Azure AD app. Found on the app's Overview page.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: Client Secret
fields.description: The client secret value generated under Certificates & secrets. Shown only once — copy it immediately after creation.

fields.name: subscription_id
fields.label: Subscription ID
fields.description: The Azure subscription ID you want Anton to monitor. Found under Subscriptions in the Azure Portal.

fields.name: log_analytics_workspace_id
fields.label: Log Analytics Workspace ID
fields.description: Optional. The workspace ID for Log Analytics / Application Insights queries. Found in your Log Analytics workspace → Overview → Workspace ID.

fields.name: cloud_environment
fields.label: Cloud Environment
fields.description: Select the Azure cloud environment your subscription belongs to.

methods.label: Managed Identity / Bearer Token
methods.description: Paste a short-lived Azure AD bearer token obtained from a managed identity or az CLI. Useful for testing or environments where Anton runs inside Azure with managed identity access.
methods.how_to:
## Managed Identity / Bearer Token

Use this method if Anton runs inside an Azure resource (VM, App Service, Container App) with a managed identity assigned, or if you want to paste a token obtained via the Azure CLI for quick testing.

## Obtaining a token via Azure CLI

1. Install and sign in to the [Azure CLI](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/install-azure-cli): `az login`.
2. Run: `az account get-access-token --resource https://management.azure.com/`
3. Copy the `accessToken` value from the JSON output.
4. Paste it into the **Bearer Token** field below along with your **Subscription ID** and **Tenant ID**.

## Using a managed identity (inside Azure)

1. Assign a **system-assigned** or **user-assigned** managed identity to the Azure resource running Anton.
2. Grant the identity the **Monitoring Reader** role on the target subscription or resource group.
3. Retrieve a token from the instance metadata endpoint: `http://169.254.169.254/metadata/identity/oauth2/token?api-version=2018-02-01&resource=https://management.azure.com/`
4. Paste the returned token below.

## Notes

- Azure AD bearer tokens expire after ~1 hour. You will need to refresh and re-paste the token periodically.
- For long-lived access, use the **Service Principal** method instead.

fields.name: bearer_token
fields.label: Bearer Token
fields.description: A valid Azure AD access token for https://management.azure.com/. Expires after ~1 hour.

fields.name: tenant_id
fields.label: Tenant ID
fields.description: Your Azure Active Directory tenant ID.

fields.name: subscription_id
fields.label: Subscription ID
fields.description: The Azure subscription ID you want Anton to monitor.

fields.name: cloud_environment
fields.label: Cloud Environment
fields.description: Select the Azure cloud environment your subscription belongs to.
